sous_titres.text: My name is Maya Tolstoy, and I'm a marine seismologist

sous_titres.text: and particularly I study the earthquakes that these volcanoes make.

sous_titres.text: Those are instruments that listen to earthquakes

sous_titres.text: and to see into the ocean crust, the way that light lets you see on the surface.

sous_titres.text: How could there be a whole ecosystem living without sunlight?
And not just living, but thriving!

sous_titres.text: Unbelievable,
This is the most insane amount of biomass I ever saw in my life,
Wow,

sous_titres.text: And they're right there in the flow, just enjoying the hydrothermal fluid,
Oh, look at 'em swarming, They love it in the smoke,
Oh, yeah,

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0162_time_2430.00s-2445.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: The vents were providing the energy for life.
It was coming from chemicals dissolved in the water,
coming from inside the Earth itself.
Not photosynthesis, but chemosynthesis.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0163_time_2445.00s-2460.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: It was a whole new basis for life, one that didn't need the sun,
only water and heat.
That party's been going on down there in the dark for the last billion years,

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0164_time_2460.00s-2475.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: and it's gonna be going on for the next billion years,

Exploring and discovering ecosystems like this,

sous_titres.text: which may not depend on energy from the sun,
opens up all sorts of interesting possibilities

sous_titres.text: That's pretty profound.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0167_time_2505.00s-2520.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: In fact, NASA is planning a mission called the Jupiter Icy Moons Orbiter.
AKA JIMO.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0168_time_2520.00s-2535.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: At 120 feet long it's gonna be several times longer
than any planetary probe ever launched,
and its nuclear reactor will power ion engines and a big science radar.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0169_time_2535.00s-2550.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: The main goal of JIMO will be to use that radar
to look down through the ice of Jupiter's three largest moons.
First, it will study Callisto, and then it'll move to Ganymede.
And these are bizarre moons, the largest of Jupiter's 61 known moons.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0170_time_2550.00s-2565.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: Callisto and Ganymede both have crusts of ancient ice and rock
that may hide oceans miles below the surface.
Further in is Io, which is a moon of fire, not ice.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0171_time_2565.00s-2580.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: Io's eccentric orbit causes it to deform as it circles Jupiter.
This intense tidal pumping pulls at the moon like taffy,
generating friction at the core which then becomes heat,

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0172_time_2580.00s-2595.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: and this heat drives the solar system's largest active volcanoes.
There are constant eruptions, rivers of lava,
and volcanic plumes shooting hundreds of miles into space.

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0173_time_2595.00s-2610.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: But the gem of the Jovian system, at least as far as the search for life is concerned,

intervalles.video_path: analyse\videos\Aliens of the Deep (2005) 1080p BRRip x264 -YTS\interval_0174_time_2610.00s-2625.00s.mp4

sous_titres.text: is Europa.
It's here where fire and ice come together in perfect harmony.
The scientific community is relatively certain
that beneath the icy, chaotic shell of Europa, there exists a liquid water ocean,

sous_titres.text: with twice the volume of all the Earth's oceans combined.
